message:
wa-e34d9 10.5.2: add spawn_with_cx cancelled-acquire recovery test and wire it into the mux-migration-completion e2e scenario

New regression coverage for the spawn_with_cx + pool-acquire
interaction that wa-e34d9 10.5.2 depends on: a cancelled child
task that attempts to acquire a pool permit must fail its
acquire, must not restore a permit that a concurrent primary
checkout is holding, and must not count as a successful checkout
(so rate-limit / quota accounting stays correct).

crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs:

* New test
  `spawn_with_cx_cancelled_pool_acquire_fails_and_healthy_retry_recovers`
  that:
  - Builds a RuntimeFixture::current_thread() runtime and a healthy
    Cx + a user-cancelled Cx (via the existing `healthy_cx()` and
    `user_cancelled_cx()` helpers).
  - Creates a `MockPool::new(1)` with exactly one permit and a
    `MockMuxClient` with a single pane (id 19).
  - Acquires the pool's only permit via a primary checkout on the
    healthy Cx, and asserts the first mux read returns the seeded
    content and `pool.available_permits() == 0`.
  - Spawns a child task via `runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx(
      &cancelled, |child_cx| async move { pool.acquire(&child_cx).await }
    )`, awaits the JoinHandle, and asserts:
      * the inner `Result` is `Err(...)`,
      * the error string contains one of
        `"cancelled" | "semaphore" | "Cancelled"` so the assertion
        is resilient to minor error-message wording drift but still
        proves cancellation (not a generic pool-empty) is what
        fired,
      * `pool.available_permits() == 0` — the cancelled acquire
        did not restore a permit that the primary checkout is
        still holding,
      * `pool.total_acquired() == 1` — the cancelled acquire did
        not count as a successful checkout (would have skewed
        quota counters),
      * `client.request_count() == 1` — the cancelled acquire
        failed before issuing an extra mux request.
  - Follow-up healthy-retry path (already in the test's next
    block) verifies that after dropping the primary checkout, a
    fresh acquire on a healthy Cx proceeds normally.

tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_5_2_mux_migration_completion.sh:

* New structural presence check in the "test file sanity" phase:
  record pass/fail for
  `spawn_with_cx_cancelled_pool_acquire_fails_and_healthy_retry_recovers`
  using the existing `record_structural_pass` /
  `record_structural_fail` helpers (fail code `E_SPAWN_WITH_CX`,
  field `spawn_with_cx_recovery_test_presence`) so the scenario
  surfaces a missing test as a first-class failure rather than
  letting `cargo test` smoke-through the undetected absence.
* New `run_rch_phase "spawn_with_cx_recovery_targeted" ...` step
  that rebuilds and runs just this test (`--test-threads=1`,
  features `asupersync-runtime,vendored`) before the full
  mux_migration_completion suite, so operators iterating on the
  spawn_with_cx behavior get fast, targeted feedback.

Test + scenario only; no production source changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -185,6 +185,12 @@ else
   record_structural_fail "timeout_recovery_test_presence" "missing" "E_TIMEOUT_RECOVERY" "${STRUCTURAL_FILE}"
 fi
 
+if rg -q 'spawn_with_cx_cancelled_pool_acquire_fails_and_healthy_retry_recovers' "${TEST_FILE}"; then
+  record_structural_pass "spawn_with_cx_recovery_test_presence" "present" "${STRUCTURAL_FILE}"
+else
+  record_structural_fail "spawn_with_cx_recovery_test_presence" "missing" "E_SPAWN_WITH_CX" "${STRUCTURAL_FILE}"
+fi
+
 FAULT_REFS=$(rg -c 'SimulatedNetwork|fault|hostile|recovery' "${TEST_FILE}" || true)
 if [[ "${FAULT_REFS}" -ge 6 ]]; then
   record_structural_pass "fault_recovery_refs" "present" "${STRUCTURAL_FILE}" "refs=${FAULT_REFS}"
@@ -225,6 +231,11 @@ run_rch_phase \
   "cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_timeout_cascade_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1" \
   test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_timeout_cascade_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1
 
+run_rch_phase \
+  "spawn_with_cx_recovery_targeted" \
+  "cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored spawn_with_cx_cancelled_pool_acquire_fails_and_healthy_retry_recovers -- --test-threads=1" \
+  test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored spawn_with_cx_cancelled_pool_acquire_fails_and_healthy_retry_recovers -- --test-threads=1
+
 echo ""
 echo "=== Phase 3: Full mux migration completion suite ==="
 run_rch_phase \
